content:
Welcome back fellow hackers! 

After a long COVID hiatus, the Rockne Invitational Golf Tournament is back in business! 

Keep on scrolling and you'll find some general news and information about this year's Rockne Invitational Golf Tournament.
title: Back by Popular Demand ...

content:
For those that got the email notice (and bothered to read it), you already know that we aren't doing "traditional" trophies this tournament. 

For the rest of you illiterates ...

---

... here's the situation.  We have been parked at a $60 entry fee per player for quite a while now.  We think it's a pretty good deal, as for most of you, that comes to about fifty cents per stroke.

But financially we haven't been hitting the break even point for a number of years.  That's ok, because there is some reserve to fall back on.  Still, we are always looking for ways to pinch pennies.  (On that sad note, I'd advise you not asking Leon what's in the sausage.)

So what to do?  Go up on prices?  We could.  But then I thought of an idea.  What if we get rid of trophies?  

Right now we spend about a third of our tournament budget on those things your wives (or husbands) won't even let you put on the mantle.  Most end up in the attic or buried in the back of the closet.  So it seems like a good place to save a little money.

Now I know what you are probably thinking: "What's a Rockne Invitational Golf Tournament without trophies?" 

I couldn't agree more. 

And so, we will, in fact, have "trophies" of sorts.  Moreover, not only will it will be a good conversation piece, but I think it will be something your wives (or husbands) may actually let you use.  

The winners of our tournament this year will each get a Rockne Invitational Golf Tournament koozie (with an ice cold beer, or root beer for the younger folks).  The koozie design, front and back will look like this:


Think of it as a mobile trophy. Its year-long bragging rights in a can or bottle! 

Feel free to use liberally and rub your friends’ noses in it. :)  

It's the same basic design for 2nd and 3rd place. 

Now, we won’t be leaving out last place.  Oh no.  Our most challenged team will get koozie keepsakes as well.  Only theirs will look like this:


Not quite a horse's ass, but it still has a bite wouldn't you say?

Anyway, we'll give a try this go around.  If the collective wants trophies next year, we do trophies.  Please do give me your feedback.
title: No "Traditional" Trophies This Time Around

content:
Believe it or not, it was May of 2019 since we last gathered to sweat buckets and swear at a little white ball. 

So this has been a while coming.  And it got me reminiscing about tournaments past. 

While there are plenty of fun and humorous memories to go around, there is one that still makes me laugh every time I think about it ...

---

Our little tournament has been going on for quite a while, so long in fact that when it started many of us played with woods that -- get this -- were actually made out of wood! 

Back then, your golf bag probably contained a motley collection of mismatched hand-me-down clubs.  And this was if you had any clubs at all -- which is an appropriate lead in to this memorable Rockne Golf Tournament moment.

The tournament on this particular year was being held in Gonzalez (just to give you a sense of how long ago this was).  One of our players back then was a man named Charlie Alexander, a person whom, I'm sure, most of you know well. 

Well, Charlie strolls into the pro shop at the Gonzales golf course and announces to the golf pro behind the counter, "I need to rent your finest set of left handed golf clubs please".  The golf pro frowns ands says, "I'm sorry sir, we don't have any left handed clubs for rent" -- to which Charlie instantly answers back "I need to rent your finest set of right handed golf clubs please".

Ole Charlie went on to set numerous course records that day.  And not one of them was good.

What is the moral of this story? 

Well, there's probably going to be a fair amount of frustration to go around today.  I am guessing that many of you probably haven't picked up a club since the last Rockne Invitational two years ago.  So my advice to you today is that when adversity strikes -- and it will strike -- to just sit back and channel your inner Charlie ...

When life makes you play with wrong handed clubs, you simply roll with the punches, and enjoy the hell out of it.

And just remember that when that adversity does strike, we're not laughing at you; we are laughing with you.
title: Tales of Tournaments Past

content:
We probably sound like a broken record on this one, but the Rockne Invitational Golf Tournament is a members only tournament. 

So what does it take to get an invite?  Basically you have to have a Rockne connection.  More specifically ...  

---

The membership qualifications (as set forth by our crack legal team) are as follows:

  1. You currently live in, or near, Rockne.
  2. Your name appears in the big red Rockne book.
  3. You are a parent, spouse, child, grandchild or great grandchild of a person meeting condition 1 or 2.
  4. You have been grandfathered in.  (You must  have played in at least 6 Rockne Invitationals to qualify for this status.)

NOTE: A five-member Rockne Invitational Golf Tournament committee has been appointed to consider all new applicants if there is any question about potential membership.
title: Membership Rules

content:
There are a few ground rules for our tournament, such as rule #5:

You are allowed to improve your lie.

No, this has nothing to do with putting down a bogey when you really got a double-par! 

If you thought that, you might should check out all of our rules and their explanations here ...

---

  1. Membership.  The Rockne Invitational Golf Tournament is a members-only event. Teams must consist of valid members who have signed up in advance to play. Membership rules apply.
  2. Two-Man Teams.  Two player teams are allowed.  You will simply play a two-man scramble instead of a three-man scramble. Do not alternate for the third shot.  There will be no third shot. Instead, your handicap will be adjusted to reflect the fact that you are only playing with 2 players.
  3. Tee Off.  Men shall play from the white tees.  Women and anyone under 12 years of age may play from the red tees.  Anyone over 65 years of age may play from the senior tees.
  4. Ball Placement.  All shots from the fairway must be played from within one full club length of the selected best ball.  On chips and putts please place the ball as close as possible to the original position of the selected shot.  Balls may not be moved from sand traps or water hazards.
  5. Improving Your Lie.  You may improve the lie of your ball (i.e. how it sits) by adjusting it with your club head in the fairway or rough.  You may not do so in sand traps.
  6. Swing and Miss?  A swing and miss does not count as a shot.  Swing until you hit it.  (It is the Rockne Invitational after all.)
  7. Gimmees.  Inside the leather on a putt is considered a gimmee.  Pick up your ball and count a stroke to speed up play.
  8. Course Rules in Effect.  Please respect all course rules.
title: Tournament Rules

content:
I do want to thank everyone who makes this tournament possible ...

Thank you to our wonderful Master of Ceremonies, Elmer Goertz.  It is a mainstay at our little tournament to hear Elmer go on about "my cousin" this, my "brother" that, "my niece" what's-her-name, and "my nephew the proctologist".  I think we all dig it.  It wouldn't be the Rockne Invitational Golf Tournament without him.

Thanks to Leon Prasek for the fine food and fixings he serves up.  Really hard to beat what he does.  

And finally, thanks to all of you who come out and play in our little tournament.  Best of luck out there today.
title: Last But Not Least ...

content:
There is happy.

There is ecstatic.

There is on-top-of-the-world with unbridled joy.

And well beyond all of this there is ...

---

... Elmer the night before the Rockne Invitational Golf Tournament!


He's not the only reason I do this.  But he's a big reason.

That's my dude.
title: If This Isn't a Portrait of Happiness, I Don't Know What Is ...

content:
Here are some helpful links to help you get around:

Team Login - The first thing you will want to do is log in as your team.  

Electronic Scorecard - Once you have logged in, you'll have access to your team's electronic scorecard. 

Tournament Results - When the tournament is over, you can get the full standings here. 

You can also find these same links in the navigation bar at the top of the screen.  Just click on the "hamburger" button.
title: Useful Links

content:
Closest to the Hole and Longest Drive holes will be the same as always, but there will be a change to how you record them ...

---

In the past, if you had the Closest to the Hole tee shot or Longest Drive, you'd write your name on a placard and place it where your ball landed.  This year we still have the placards, but you'll record your name directly on the electronic scorecard.

For eligible Closest to the Hole and Longest Drive holes, you'll see an extra button pop up on the electronic scorecard:

Simply click the button.  You'll see a dialog that has your team member names on it:

You will (1) click the name of the player with the superlative shot, and (2) click the Enter button.

Then, if you scroll down on the electronic scorecard, you'll actually be able to see tracker for each Closest to the Hole and Longest Drive hole:

The tracker will show you who is currently winning the hole, and who has been supplanted from that position.
title: A Change to How We Do Closest to the Hole and Longest Drive

content:
Teams will record scores for each hole on their electronic scorecard.  If you navigate to your team's scorecard page, you will see a Scorecard section:

The Scorecard section features:

  1. A table showing how many holes the team has played and how many remain, the team's raw score (in par relative format), the team's handicap, and the team's adjusted scored (in par relative format).
  2. A vertical bar chart giving a graphical view of how many holes have been played vs. how many remain.
  3. Scorecard fill-in boxes for each hole showing the hole number, par, the distance from the white tees, and the par relative team score if one has been entered.
  4. A button bar.

The Scorecard automatically traverses to the team's start hole when the view is displayed.  

To enter a score for that hole, click the Input Score button:


You will see a dialog that allows you to enter the score for the hole:

Simply (1) click the par relative or raw score input button and (2) click Enter.  When you do this, the score will automatically be posted, and your scorecard will automatically increment to the next hole.

You can go to a different hole if needed.  There are several ways to do this:

You may swipe left or right on the scorecard inputs (I know many of you will be familiar with how to do this) and click to select a hole:

Or you can use the previous / next hole buttons:

Or you can click the Go to Hole button:

When a scored is entered, the Leader Board will be updated.  You can get to the Leader Board and view the current standings by scrolling down the page to the Leader Board section:

The Leader Board tracks all teams registering scores.  Your team will be in bold print and highlighted in gray. 

You can click on the team name of any team in the table and find out more information about the team including who the team members are.

As noted, the Leader Board is automatically updated whenever you enter a score.  But if you want to refresh the view without entering a score, simply click the Refresh button at the bottom of the page.

NOTE: The Leader Board will be disabled with approximately six holes to play.  Hey, the has to be some suspense right?
title: The Electronic Scorecard
